sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: The upcoming clash between Empoli and Lazio is set for May 4, 2025, at 10:30 AM in the Carlo Castellani Stadium in Empoli. This Round 35 encounter sees Empoli, currently languishing at 19th in the league with just 25 points, hosting Lazio, who are in a much stronger 7th position with 60 points. With Empoli fighting to avoid relegation and Lazio aiming to solidify their mid-table standing, this match promises a significant contrast in form and ambition. The referee, Andrea Colombo, is known for a high card count, averaging 5.1 yellow cards and 0.33 red cards per game, which could influence the game’s physicality.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at the head-to-head record, Lazio hold a dominant position over Empoli in their last 5 matchups, securing 4 wins with 1 draw and no victories for Empoli. Their most recent encounter on October 6, 2024, ended with Lazio winning 2-1 at home, with Mattia Zaccagni earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.6 Sofascore rating. Lazio have also been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 meetings against Empoli, highlighting their tendency to take early control in these fixtures. Additionally, matches between these sides often see high card counts, with over 4.5 cards in 5 of their last 6 games, and corner counts frequently exceeding 10.5 in the same span.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Empoli are in a dire run of form, currently on a 10-match winless streak and without a clean sheet in their last 4 games. They’ve also been the first to concede in 7 of their last 9 matches, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. On the other hand, Lazio are unbeaten in their last 4 matches across all competitions and have been the first to score in 5 of their last 7 games. However, their recent away games have often seen under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 7 matches, suggesting a more cautious approach on the road compared to Empoli’s home games, where over 2.5 goals have occurred in 5 of their last 6.

sections.subtitle: Empoli’s Key Players and Challenges
sections.text: Empoli will rely heavily on forward Sebastiano Esposito, who has scored 8 goals this season with a notable expected goals (xG) of 5.46, overperforming by 2.542. Defenders Mattia De Sciglio and Ardian Ismajli stand out with high league ranks, De Sciglio winning 75% of aerial duels (5th in the league) and Ismajli securing 69.8% of ground duels (also 5th). Midfielder Giuseppe Pezzella, with 10 big chances created (4th in the league), is another key creator despite not scoring. However, Empoli are hampered by missing players like Christian Kouamé and Alberto Grassi, which could weaken their attack and midfield stability.

sections.subtitle: Lazio’s Standout Performers
sections.text: Lazio boast several in-form players, notably midfielder Mattia Zaccagni, who ranks 3rd in the league for average rating (7.37) and is often fouled, averaging 3 times per game (also 3rd). Forward Valentín Castellanos leads their attack with 10 goals from an xG of 12.75, supported by Boulaye Dia and Pedro, each with 8 goals. Defender Samuel Gigot impresses with a 77.3% aerial duel win rate (3rd in the league), providing defensive solidity. Mattéo Guendouzi, a standout against Empoli historically with an average Sofascore rating of 7.57 over recent matches, will also be crucial despite scoring just 1 goal in those games.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights: Empoli
sections.text: Empoli’s season stats reflect their struggles, with only 27 goals scored (18th in the league) and 54 conceded (16th for least goals conceded). Their average ball possession is a mere 40.7% (19th), and they rank poorly in big chances created (48, 18th) and converted (33.3%, 16th). However, they excel defensively in some areas, leading the league in tackles (18.65 per game), interceptions (8.79 per game), and clearances (26.74 per game). Their goal distribution shows a tendency to score late, with 29.6% of goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes.

sections.subtitle: Statistical Insights: Lazio
sections.text: Lazio’s attacking output is more balanced, with their goal distribution showing strength in the latter stages, as 24.4% of their goals come between the 76th and 90th minutes, and another 24.4% between the 46th and 60th minutes. While specific season stats for Lazio’s goals scored aren’t detailed here, their players’ individual contributions, like Castellanos’ 10 goals, indicate a potent attack. Their recent form player, goalkeeper Christos Mandas, has an impressive average Sofascore rating of 7.64 over the last 5 matches, suggesting reliability between the sticks.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Matchup and Game Expectations
sections.text: Empoli’s defensive strengths in tackles and interceptions could frustrate Lazio’s attacking fluidity, especially given Lazio’s tendency for lower-scoring away games. However, Empoli’s poor possession stats (40.7%) and high foul count (506, 2nd most in the league) may invite pressure and set pieces, areas where Lazio’s aerial threats like Samuel Gigot could capitalize. Lazio’s early scoring tendency (first to score in 5 of 7 recent games) might test Empoli’s shaky defense, which has conceded first in 7 of 9 matches. Expect a physical game with referee Andrea Colombo’s high card average of 5.1 per game likely to come into play.
